title: ‘It was a set-up, we were fooled’: the coalmine that ate an Indian village – podcast
author: Written by Ankur Paliwal and read by Raj Ghatak. Produced by Jessica Beck. The executive producer was Ellie Bury
standfirst: In a pristine forest in central India, the multibillion-dollar mining giant Adani has razed trees – and homes – to dig more coal. How does this kind of destruction get the go-ahead? By Ankur Paliwal
body_text: Archive: NDTV; Heritage Times Read text version here

people_mentioned.name: Ankur Paliwal
people_mentioned.role: Author and journalist who wrote the article

people_mentioned.name: Raj Ghatak
people_mentioned.role: Narrator who reads the podcast version

time_periods.label: Contemporary period of Adani mining expansion in India

cross_domain_bridges: This mining conflict illustrates how environmental destruction intersects with economic policy, as government approvals for extractive industries reveal the tension between development models and ecological preservation. The case demonstrates how corporate power operates within legal frameworks, showing how regulatory capture and policy-making processes can override local community rights and environmental protections.

film_adaptation.pitch: Villagers in central India fight back against mining giant Adani as the company destroys their pristine forest home to extract coal, revealing how local communities are steamrolled by corporate power.
